title: equisetum serotinum
intro: Equisetum serotinum, also known as the late horsetail, is a perennial plant that belongs to the Equisetaceae family and is one of the oldest living plant species. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its high silica and mineral content, which are believed to have antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Equisetum serotinum include treating kidney stones, urinary tract infections, and skin conditions such as acne and eczema. Equisetum serotinum is typically prepared as a tea or infusion, and it can also be dried and used as a powder or capsule in herbal supplements.
